_meta.description: Layer 4 prompt fragments for province-level (game Province) summarization. LLM rewrites ONLY content tags — address tags (world/nation/region/province/district/locality) are FACTS assigned at capture, preserved by layer code, never touched by the LLM.
design_principles: P1: Simple language. A Sonnet-class LLM needs CLARITY over PRECISION.
P2: No internal mechanics (formulas, multipliers, balance constants).
P3: No flavor or lore. Fragments are CONTEXT, not worldbuilding.
P4: Province summaries are BROADER than district summaries. Think provincial trends, not individual events.
P5: Dates matter. 'when' attributes tell you how recent each event is. Weight recent events more heavily.
P6: Layer 2 supporting-detail events add granularity. Use them to sharpen the narrative, not to list.
P7: You rewrite CONTENT tags only — never add, drop, rename, or reorder address tags (world:, nation:, region:, province:, district:, locality:). Those are facts about where events happened.

_l4_core: You write province-level summaries from district reports. Each district has already been summarized. Your job is to synthesize these district summaries into a single provincial overview. Focus on: what activities dominate this province, how districts relate to each other, and whether conditions are stable or changing. Write 2-4 sentences. Use the province and district names provided. Recent events (today, yesterday) matter more than old ones.
_l4_output:
Respond with JSON only. Fields:
- "narrative": 2-4 sentence provincial overview synthesized from the district reports below.
- "tags": IMPORTANT — You are rewriting the CONTENT tags only. Do NOT include any address tags (world:, nation:, region:, province:, district:, locality:) — those are facts that the system assigns automatically from where events happened. Your job:
  1. Collect all CONTENT tags (everything that is not an address tag) from the input events.
  2. Keep 66-80% of those tags — drop only the ones that are irrelevant to your narrative.
  3. Reorder the remaining tags by relevance to your narrative (most relevant first).
  4. Add any new tags from these Layer 4 categories that clearly apply:
     urgency_level: none, low, moderate, high, critical, emergency
     event_status: emerging, developing, ongoing, resolving, resolved, recurring, escalating
     player_impact: player_driven, partially_player, world_driven, mixed
  5. Also use these interpretive categories from lower layers:
     sentiment: positive, negative, neutral, dangerous, prosperous, declining, hopeful, grim
     trend: increasing, decreasing, stable, volatile, emerging, accelerating
     intensity: light, moderate, heavy, extreme
     resource_status: abundant, steady, scarce, critical, depleted, recovering
  The first tag in your list should be the MOST relevant to the narrative. Tag order matters.

Example: {"narrative": "Western Province is dominated by mining activity. Resource extraction is straining iron supplies while combat with cave fauna persists.", "tags": ["domain:gathering", "domain:combat", "intensity:heavy", "resource_status:scarce", "urgency_level:moderate", "event_status:developing", "player_impact:player_driven", "sentiment:prosperous", "trend:accelerating", "species:cave_spider"]}
l4_context:province_summary: This is a province-level summary. Districts are subdivisions of this province. Events tagged cross-district are patterns that span multiple districts (behavioral profiles, faction trends). Supporting-detail events are granular Layer 2 observations included because they are highly relevant to the district summaries above. Each event is shown with its full tag list in [brackets] for context — but you rewrite CONTENT tags only, never address tags.
l4_example:province:
EXAMPLE INPUT:
<province name="Western Province">
  <district name="Deep Mine District">
    <event category="regional_synthesis" severity="significant" when="2 days ago" tags="[province:province_1, district:district_3, domain:gathering, domain:combat, intensity:heavy, resource_status:scarce, species:cave_spider]">The Deep Mine District shows heavy resource extraction with node depletion, while combat with cave spiders persists.</event>
    <event category="cross_domain" severity="moderate" when="5 days ago" tags="[province:province_1, district:district_3, domain:combat, domain:gathering, trend:emerging]">Combat and resource extraction co-occurring in Deep Mine.</event>
  </district>
  <district name="Western Frontier">
    <event category="regional_synthesis" severity="moderate" when="today" tags="[province:province_1, district:district_1, domain:combat, domain:gathering, intensity:moderate, sentiment:dangerous]">The Western Frontier has moderate combat in Whispering Woods and concentrated gathering in Iron Hills.</event>
  </district>
  <cross-district>
    <event category="player_identity" severity="moderate" when="today" tags="[domain:gathering, domain:combat, scope:global, trend:stable]">The player is primarily a gatherer with combatant tendencies, concentrated in one province.</event>
  </cross-district>
</province>

EXAMPLE OUTPUT (content tags only — no address tags):
{"narrative": "Western Province is dominated by mining activity concentrated in the Deep Mine District and Western Frontier. Resource extraction is straining iron supplies, particularly in Deep Mine where combat with cave fauna accompanies intensive gathering. The player's activity drives most of this pressure, focused on resource collection with combat as a byproduct.", "tags": ["domain:gathering", "domain:combat", "intensity:heavy", "resource_status:scarce", "urgency_level:moderate", "event_status:developing", "player_impact:player_driven", "species:cave_spider", "sentiment:dangerous", "trend:accelerating"]}
